title: Adding Schema
content_md:
# Adding Schema

- Code: b2ebc7f8
- Source: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=b2ebc7f86ec3466a9c58243955893339
- Video: https://www.loom.com/share/60819ad50c8649f0b89911f743c38fe3?sid=8369b2c8-a9c2-416d-bbed-ce235faae347?hide_owner=true&hide_share=true&hide_title=true&hideEmbedTopBar=true
- Duration: 3m 39s
- Resources:
  - [Schema Markup Testing Tool](https://validator.schema.org/)

## Description

_(no description)_

transcript.text: So let's talk about schema. Schema is easy to overlook but it is helpful and it
is incredibly incredibly fast to do. So there's no reason that every single
URL on your domain should not have schema. It definitely should. So what schema is
is it's a markup language structured data that helps Google and other search
engines understand what the content is. Okay so the types of schema, the big main
types I'm going to cover here, organization schema, local business,
product, article, FAQ, review and breadcrumb. So let's break these down one
by one. So organization schema should be basically on every single URL on your
domain on the header of every page and it's there to provide Google search
engines with consistent information about your business across the entire
domain. Local business schema that should only be on one URL and it should be on
the URL that your Google business profile points to. So if you have multiple
locations, each location page should have schema that matches the Google
business profile. If you only have one location it's probably going to be your
homepage and your homepage then should have local business schema that matches
your Google business profile. And this improves local SEO by showing Google
confirming that your business on the landing page is the exact same business
on the Google business profile. So all the data, all the information needs to
match. The next type is product schema. Obviously this is mainly for e-commerce
type websites and this would go on an individual product page and that's going
to provide the information about the product price availability specifications.
Just more information so Google, Google can do a pretty good job guessing by
looking at your page but by using structured data you're going to make
sure that Google isn't guessing. Article schema should be on every single blog
post, every single article and the goal of that is if it's done correctly it can
actually make the pages look better in search results which drive improved
click-through rate. FAQ schema should be on any page with FAQs which should be a
lot of your pages if you're following along. The reason we want to do that is
Google can then display questions and answers directly on the search engine
results page and the more space that your result takes up on the SERP the more
clicks you're going to get. Review schema that goes on every page that has reviews.
Sometimes you can get the review ratings to display directly that's getting
harder and harder to do but regardless if you have customer reviews you should
have review schema. Now breadcrumb schema basically you'll put that on every page
that has a hierarchical structure so the big example for hierarchical structure
are target URLs and supporting content and the reason for that is to make sure
that Google understands the site structure and that these are silos that
you've created. So breadcrumb schema should definitely be everywhere that you
are implementing a hierarchical site structure. So schema should be on every
URL on the domain. Use schema on every URL you should. I'm Yoda here so it should
be on every URL even if it's just organizational schema because nothing
else fits it should still be there. You can put multiple types on there so if
you have an article with an FAQ section that has reviews you're gonna have the
review schema the FAQ schema the article schema the organizational schema and
the breadcrumb schema okay. So you always need to validate with Google's
structured data testing tool that link is in the module and chat GPT will write
the schema for you. You can just take that and then paste it into your website.
The prompts to get chat GPT to write it for you super super simple they're also
in the module the prompts that we use.
